message:
fix(storage): fail closed when autocommit_retain cannot be disabled

The \`FrankenStorage\` bootstrap path must disable
\`autocommit_retain\` on the underlying frankensqlite connection so
a long-lived MVCC connection does not accumulate unbounded write
snapshots. The old code tried two PRAGMA variants in order
(\`PRAGMA fsqlite.autocommit_retain = OFF;\` then
\`PRAGMA autocommit_retain = OFF;\`), but when BOTH variants
failed it only emitted a warn-level log and kept the connection
anyway. Any deployment on a frankensqlite build without either
PRAGMA therefore silently ran with retained autocommit snapshots —
a correctness bug with no operator-visible trigger until MVCC
memory pressure showed up much later.

Extract the retry loop into a reusable helper and switch the
failure mode from \"warn and continue\" to \"return an error up to
the bootstrap caller\". Production callers will now fail to open
storage when autocommit_retain cannot be disabled, which is the
safe outcome — the alternative is a corrupted long-running process.

Changes:

- New module-level constant
  \`AUTOCOMMIT_RETAIN_OFF_PRAGMAS: [&str; 2]\` lists the two PRAGMA
  variants in preferred order. Used by both the production path
  and the new tests so the list is single-sourced.

- New generic helper
  \`disable_autocommit_retain<E>(execute: impl FnMut(&'static str)
  -> Result<(), E>) -> Result<&'static str>\`. Walks
  \`AUTOCOMMIT_RETAIN_OFF_PRAGMAS\` in order, short-circuits on the
  first Ok and returns the pragma text that worked, logs debug-
  level context on each failure, and on exhaustion returns an
  anyhow::Error whose message includes both the refusal sentence
  (\"refusing to keep a long-lived MVCC connection that may
  accumulate unbounded write snapshots\") and the text of every
  attempted PRAGMA plus the underlying error. That refusal string
  is the stable marker callers can grep for in logs.

- Replace the in-line loop inside \`FrankenStorage\`'s PRAGMA
  application with a \`disable_autocommit_retain(|pragma|
  self.conn.execute(pragma).map(|_| ()))\` call, use \`?\` so
  bootstrap failures propagate up, and emit a single debug-level
  \"disabled frankensqlite autocommit_retain for storage
  connection\" line with the selected PRAGMA when it succeeds.

Tests added in the same file's \`mod tests\`:

- \`autocommit_retain_disable_tries_compat_then_canonical_pragma\`
  uses a stubbed executor that fails on the compat PRAGMA but
  succeeds on the canonical one. Asserts the helper returns the
  canonical PRAGMA text and that both PRAGMAs were attempted in
  order — pins fallback ordering for future refactors.

- \`autocommit_retain_disable_fails_closed_when_no_pragma_works\`
  uses a stubbed executor that rejects every PRAGMA. Asserts the
  helper returns an error, that the error message contains the
  \"refusing to keep a long-lived MVCC connection\" sentence, and
  that the error message preserves both attempted PRAGMA strings
  so on-call engineers see which variants were tried.

Strict behavior tightening — no flag, no opt-out. The old
warn-and-continue behavior was itself a silent landmine.
